Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 2 · active · low risk · €120,000 (flat since 2021 — NO price increase in 4+ years!) · since Jun 2021
Overview

Trade Republic is an ACTIVE German fintech/neobank account — NOT a prospect as previously classified. Customer since Jun 2021 with €120K ARR that has been FLAT for 4+ years with no price increase. Happy health, low churn risk. All teams already on Clarisights = limited upsell. Notable: Christian Hecker (CEO!) is a contact. Marcus Letsch is champion (took over from Eva Wilke in 2023). Currently working on new OBC calculation based on incrementality factors.

Timeline
  • Jun 2021: Customer since
  • Invited to AppsFlyer joint dinner in Berlin
  • Philip
Key Quotes

None.

No specific product issues flagged.

Welltech $180K New Business Closed Won Owner 484225689 2023-11-03 2024-07-25 131
Renewal for Welltech - Year 3 $222K Renewals At Risk Arun / Avi 2024-12-06 2026-04-02 15

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 2 · at-risk · high risk · $180,000 → $222,000 at renewal · since Jul 2024
Overview

Welltech is a global health/fitness account that is AT CRITICAL RISK. Since Jul 2024 with $180K ARR jumping to $222K at renewal (May 31, 2026). Exec champions (Natalia & Sigal) have left. Only Ahmed Koraiem remains. Ad-hoc engagement only — no regular QBR cadence. Their internal data pipelines are now better than Clarisights. Arun Srinivasan (CEO) is the CXO on Clarisights side.

Timeline
  • Jul 2024: Customer since (pilot started Mar 2024)
  • Active with opt-out clause after Year 1 (Mar 31 deadline — PAST)
  • Wants hourly data syncs — current daily sync insufficient
  • Project Neo timelines shared loosely — expectation risk
  • Avi flagged as "unclear state"
  • Gold standard demo report set built for Welltech (Cross Channel, Search, Social, App/Mobile)
  • Feb 2026: Reviewed in EU Top Accounts with Sasha — flagged HIGH churn
  • May 31, 2026: Renewal deadline. 30-day notice = April 30
Key Quotes
  • Demo report insight: "The killer thing is to be able to tell marketing teams how they can improve performance by quickly looking at insights." (from Welltech demo playbook)
  • Notion: "Analytics team cannot work with Clarisights — works with internal data instead"
  • Notion: "Marketing team complains about lack of visibility when data is out/not updated"
Product Issues
  • Data freshness: Wants hourly syncs. Once-daily insufficient. Mozek V3 migration needed
  • Integration gaps: During pilot, brought up integration requirements Clarisights doesn't support
  • Data harmonization: Their internal curated datasets are more harmonized than Clarisights output
  • Creative reporting: 4 specialized datasets needed — complex requirements

Renewal for Renewal for THREE.E - Year 3 $30K Renewals At Risk Arun / Avi 2025-06-24 2026-03-26 9

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 2 · churning · high risk · $32,388
Overview

Three.e is a performance marketing agency in Germany that is CHURNING. The mood towards Clarisights within the team is "very bad" due to data trust issues — numbers didn't match, and they had to stop using the dashboard. Their only client using Clarisights (Goldner) is switching to Minubo for data setup, which will also provide a marketing dashboard as side effect. Three.e previously served as both customer AND partner (spoke at Exactag events). The churn represents loss of both partnership AND customer revenue.

Timeline
  • Was active customer + event partner (Femke and Marisa spoke at Hamburg Exactag event)
  • GA4 data outage caused data trust issues
  • Attribution fix done but damage to trust already occurred
  • Managing Madeleine on Excel still (not fully adopted)
  • "Mood towards Clarisights is very bad" — Sylvia
  • Will cancel at June renewal
  • Only client using CS (Goldner) switching to Minubo for data setup
  • Lotto24 /
Key Quotes
  • Sylvia: "Currently the mood towards Clarisights within the team is very bad."
  • Avi: "In current state of the product, customers cannot do everything by themselves." (partly about Three.e experience)
Product Issues
  • Data trust: Numbers didn't match. Had to stop using dashboard. GA4 data outage was the trigger
  • Core data reliability issue — the universal product gap hitting this account hardest

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 2 · active · medium risk · €67,200 (up from €60K — 12% increase at first renewal) · since Feb 2025
Overview

Enpal is a Tier 1 account in the German solar/energy sector that is AT RISK. The account has a complicated setup with a major pending change that hasn't been properly handed over. Key stakeholder Arne is "pretty pissed." Philip

Timeline
  • Account managed by Philip
Key Quotes
  • Internal: "Arne is pretty pissed" (about pending setup changes)
  • Philip: "We are not setting ourselves up for a great first impression if we overly emphasize creative analytics" (partly driven by Enpal experience)
Product Issues
  • Data freshness: Once-daily sync insufficient. Enpal wants intra-day data refresh. Mozek V3 migration needed
  • Creative analytics: Product falls short on critical workflows. Can't show multiple channels in single creative view (FB + Google). Oversold vs. reality
  • Complex setup: Complicated configuration with pending changes. Poor handover from Philip to Akshay

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 1 · active · medium risk
Overview

Superbet is a Tier 1 iGaming/betting account currently in onboarding across multiple markets (Romania, Poland, Brazil). The account is complex with multiple teams (SEO, Affiliate, Programmatic, Paid Social, Paid Search) needing to be onboarded. Aditya Soni manages the onboarding with Avisek Rath providing strategic oversight. Clarisights aims to replace Tableau as primary attribution/reporting tool.

Timeline
  • Onboarding kicked off with multiple teams: Raul, Felipe, Marco's team
  • Blocker: custom dimension alignment. Mitan + Andreea coordinating
  • Avisek managing before OOO (Mar 2-23, 2026)
  • Complex multi-market setup across Romania, Poland, Brazil
Key Quotes

None directly attributed.

Product Issues
  • Naming conventions: Inconsistent UTM/campaign naming across teams breaks reporting
  • Custom dimensions hierarchy: Blocker during onboarding — can't add rule below campaign level once CD value is defined at campaign level

Universal Music Group $120K New Business Closed Won Arun / Avi 2021-01-29 — 0

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 1 · active · low risk · $380,000
Overview

UMG is a Tier 1 strategic account with multi-territory deployment across US, Australia, Japan, UK, and Germany. The account is actively expanding, with Japan migrating from DOMO to Clarisights. Akshay leads the account with Shakthi supporting US/AUS territories and Philip covering JP.

Timeline
  • Active multi-territory expansion across US, AUS, JP, UK, DE
  • JP territory moving reporting from DOMO to Clarisights
  • Count aggregation (countuniqueif) feature scoped specifically for UMG US, releasing April 2026
  • Table sort behavior request from UMG user (wants a-A-z-
Key Quotes
  • Philip on count aggregation timeline: "a few weeks or several months?"
Product Issues
  • Stale dimensions: Garbage dimension values persist after rule changes, confusing first-time report builders in JP territory
  • Data groups ACL: Campaign-level ACL needed for multi-brand reporting in JP. Current data groups only work at account level — "practical limitations make it unusable"
  • Count aggregation: UMG US needs countuniqueif with custom metric check on spend. Other accounts (Uber, DH, AY) fine with current definition — only UMG needs this
  • Table sort: User wants a-A-z-

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 1 · active · low risk · $348,000 · since Sep 2018
Overview

Delivery Hero is a Tier 1 strategic account — the longest-running customer (since Sep 2018) with $348K ARR. Global presence including Korea expansion via Woowa Bros. Sub-brands include Hungerstation. Avisek Rath leads the account with deep institutional knowledge, supported by Adit and Aditya Soni as product specialists. Fully self-serve — no dashboards built by CS team. Quarterly cadence. Slack-based comms with separate channels per team.

Timeline
  • Sep 2018: Customer since
  • Active account with Hungerstation sub-brand
  • Woowa Bros (Korea) onboarding as pre-pilot — Korean ad channels needed (Kakao, Naver, etc.)
  • Agency contract for Woowa ends June = deadline for Clarisights decision
  • AppsFlyer joint dinner in Berlin — DH invited alongside Trade Republic, HelloFresh
  • Granular Dimensions + Metrics Evaluation document exists on Google Drive (high intel value)
  • Upsell opportunity: Glovo (was $120K account in past)
  • Renewal: Dec 31, 2026
Key Quotes

None directly attributed, but Avisek Rath's deep institutional knowledge drives DH account strategy.

Product Issues
  • Data groups ACL: Campaign-level ACL needed for multi-brand reporting. Same issue as UMG JP
  • AppsFlyer native integration: Current integration limited. Neo project will bring native AF support — critical for DH
  • Korean ad channels: Kakao, Naver, Buzzvil needed for Woowa Bros onboarding

Overview

Tier 2 · active · medium risk
Overview

Udemy is a Tier 2 EdTech account based in the US. The account is active but has medium risk due to urgent pending requests being escalated and timezone friction — India-based CS team working at 2am to support US hours. Salesforce integration is being scoped specifically for Udemy, with a demo environment shared in March 2026. Account flagged by Avi as having unanticipated complaints.

Timeline
  • Active with urgent pending requests escalated
  • Timezone friction — India team at 2am for US support
  • Salesforce integration scoped for Udemy — demo environment shared Mar 20, 2026

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 2 · active · low risk · $90,000 (up 50% from $60K — discount removed at renewal) · since Nov 2023
Overview

Parship/Lovoo is a Tier 2 dating industry account in Germany. The account is stable with Pierre as the champion, but there is price sensitivity — Pierre is "afraid to defend price increase to parent company." A new Lovoo POC has been introduced. TMG apps (Growlr, Skout, Tagged, MeetMe) are migrating onto the platform, expanding the footprint. The account also serves as a referral source for prospects like Seven.One.

Timeline
  • Stable account with Pierre as champion
  • Price increase handled but caused friction — sign-on discounts removed after 2 years
  • Pierre "afraid to defend price increase to parent company"
  • New Lovoo POC introduced
  • TMG apps migrating: Growlr, Skout, Tagged, MeetMe
  • Account used as reference for Seven.One prospect
Key Quotes
  • Pierre is "afraid to defend price increase to parent company" (price sensitivity signal)
Product Issues

No specific product issues flagged.

Overview

They want internal + AppsFlyer data models to coexist. Avi flagged the account as having "unclear state" with an "unexpected situation." Pranay Budime manages the account on CS side.

Timeline
  • Complex pilot involving SKAN + Cohort analysis
  • Piyush (decision maker) re-engaged after period of disengagement
  • Wants internal + AppsFlyer data models to coexist
  • Avi flagged as "unclear state" — unexpected situation
  • Pranay Budime managing on CS side

None directly attributed.

Product Issues
  • Cohort analysis: Cumulative metrics don't fit table well. Workaround = conditional custom metrics
  • AppsFlyer native integration: Current integration limited. Neo will bring native AF support

1 deal $60K lifetime

Timeline

Owner(s)
Arun / Avi
Open Pipeline
—

Open in HubSpot →
LOST. Free access deal cut. Reliability complaints from new CEO Laura. Introduced Audible but not worth CS cost. Product gaps: platform-reliability, data-import-delays.
DealAmountPipelineStageOwnerCreatedLast Note#NotesLost Reason
Customlytics $60K New Business Closed Won Arun / Avi 2021-01-18 2023-10-01 4

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · lost · none risk
Overview

Customlytics was a mobile marketing agency partnership where Clarisights provided free platform access in exchange for customer introductions (Deutsche Telekom, etc.). The partnership deteriorated after new CEO Laura raised reliability complaints — frequent outages, no proactive comms, data import lagging, MMP data off for some accounts (HVV). Jan decided to cut them loose rather than provide dedicated CS support for free. Before the split, Customlytics intro'd Clarisights to Audible (Director of Mobile Marketing, demo booked). The partnership loss generated Avi's devastating quote about product state.

Timeline
  • Was free access partnership — platform access in exchange for customer intros
  • WIN: Introduced Clarisights to Audible (Director Mobile Marketing, demo booked May 7)
  • New CEO Laura raised reliability complaints: frequent outages, no proactive comms, data import lagging
  • MMP data off for some accounts (HVV)
  • Jan decided to cut Customlytics loose — can't support with dedicated CS for free
  • Nov 2025: Jan reiterated to Customlytics they won't get dedicated CS support
  • Partnership terminated
Key Quotes
  • Avi: "In current state of the product, customers cannot do everything by themselves. Any user is guaranteed to need support to be able to achieve anything meaningful."
  • Jan: "I would rather cut Customlytics loose than serve them like today."
Product Issues
  • Platform reliability: Frequent outages, no proactive comms
  • Data import delays: Lagging across all workspaces. MMP data off for some accounts (HVV)

Lufthansa Group - NB 01 25 $280K New Business Closed Lost Jan Rixgens 2025-01-16 2025-11-10 109 We are currently working on different organizational target pictures and until this is clarified we will not engage in POCs.

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · prospect · none risk
Overview

Publicis (agency) and Lufthansa (enterprise brand) are being pursued as a combo deal. Demo has been scheduled. Sylvia is managing the relationship. Lufthansa is interested, and the aviation vertical represents a new industry signal for Clarisights. Agency + enterprise brand deals have different dynamics — agency becomes the operational user while the brand is the budget holder.

Timeline
  • Demo scheduled — Lufthansa interested
  • Sylvia managing the relationship
  • Agency + enterprise brand combo deal structure
  • Aviation = new vertical signal for Clarisights

Faire $60K New Business Closed Lost Arun / Avi 2023-10-30 — 0 lost contact

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · lost · none risk
Overview

Faire is a LOST deal. The champion (Lacey) used Cursor (AI coding tool) to build their own analytics, making Clarisights not commercially justifiable. The loss represents a broader threat: DIY analytics powered by AI coding tools replacing purpose-built marketing analytics platforms. Despite the loss, the signal was "not now, not never" — suggesting future re-engagement potential.

Timeline
  • Champion: Lacey (analytics team)
  • Lacey used Cursor (AI coding tool) to self-serve analytics
  • Deal lost — not commercially justifiable
  • Signal: "Not now, not never" — potential future re-engagement
Key Quotes
  • "Not now, not never." (about future potential)
Product Issues

None — lost to DIY analytics, not product gaps.

DealAmountPipelineStageOwnerCreatedLast Note#NotesLost Reason
adidas - NB 02 25 $250K New Business Closed Lost Jan Rixgens 2025-02-10 2025-08-28 40 lost contact
Adidas 2022 $300K New Business Closed Lost Arun / Avi 2022-10-31 2023-02-19 1 No first call set up
Adidas NL - Deal 2021 $10K New Business Closed Lost Owner 42481342 2020-02-10 — 0

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · prospect · none risk
Overview

Adidas is a global sportswear enterprise prospect. Arun met with the data & martech team (notably, no marketing people were present). The data team acknowledged local teams have "a lot of autonomy" with no visibility on how local teams steer budgets. They nodded when Funnel's struggles with data volumes were mentioned. Key concern: worried about being "another data silo." The meeting was facilitated by Sylvia. This is an enterprise data team sale requiring different positioning than a typical marketing team sale. Reference point: HelloFresh manages 300 users with one admin.

Timeline
  • Meeting held — Arun met data & martech team (thanks to Sylvia intro)
  • No marketing people present — data team sale, not marketing team sale
  • Data team acknowledged local teams have "a lot of autonomy"
  • Worried about being "another data silo"
  • Nodded at Funnel's data volume struggles
  • Reference: HelloFresh manages 300 users with one admin
Key Quotes
  • Arun: "Considering there was no one from Marketing involved, I thought the meeting went as well as possible."
  • Adidas data team: local teams have "a lot of autonomy" — no visibility on budget steering
Product Issues

No specific product issues flagged — but positioning needs to address "data silo" concern.

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · prospect · none risk
Overview

Audible is a global audio/entertainment prospect introduced through the Customlytics partnership. The Director of Mobile Marketing was the contact, and a demo was booked for May 7. Unclear if the demo proceeded given the Customlytics partnership was subsequently cut loose. An Amazon subsidiary, making this potentially a significant enterprise account.

Timeline
  • Introduced via Customlytics partnership
  • Demo booked May 7 with Director of Mobile Marketing
  • Status uncertain — Customlytics partnership terminated after this intro

None.

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 2 · active · low risk · $90,000 (up 50% from $60K — discount removed at renewal) · since Nov 2023
Overview

Parship/Lovoo is a Tier 2 dating industry account in Germany. The account is stable with Pierre as the champion, but there is price sensitivity — Pierre is "afraid to defend price increase to parent company." A new Lovoo POC has been introduced. TMG apps (Growlr, Skout, Tagged, MeetMe) are migrating onto the platform, expanding the footprint. The account also serves as a referral source for prospects like Seven.One.

Timeline
  • Stable account with Pierre as champion
  • Price increase handled but caused friction — sign-on discounts removed after 2 years
  • Pierre "afraid to defend price increase to parent company"
  • New Lovoo POC introduced
  • TMG apps migrating: Growlr, Skout, Tagged, MeetMe
  • Account used as reference for Seven.One prospect
Key Quotes
  • Pierre is "afraid to defend price increase to parent company" (price sensitivity signal)
Product Issues

No specific product issues flagged.

Lidl Online DE 2021 — New Business Closed Lost Owner 40361673 2020-01-21 — 0

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · prospect · none risk
Overview

Schwarz Gruppe (parent of Lidl) is a major German retail/grocery enterprise prospect. Pain points resonated at the Exactag Dusseldorf event, and a follow-up call was set. One of the largest retailers in Europe, making this a potentially massive enterprise account.

Timeline
  • Connected at Exactag Dusseldorf event
  • Pain points resonated
  • Follow-up call set
Key Quotes

None.

Product Issues

No product issues flagged (early prospect).

Extended pilot but only covering 20% of steering needs. Data discrepancy blocker. Product gaps: snowflake-integration, data-trust. Competitors: looker, ga4.
DealAmountPipelineStageOwnerCreatedLast Note#NotesLost Reason
Taxfix - NB 02 25 $63K New Business Closed Lost Jan Rixgens 2025-02-26 2025-06-04 57 not complex enough, maybe later
Taxfix 2022 $120K New Business Closed Lost Owner 476428719 2022-09-27 2023-08-02 1 Timing
Taxfix $120K New Business Closed Lost Owner 476428719 2023-07-26 2024-02-13 24 Went silent all of a sudden. Opened headcount for marketing analysts.

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · pilot · high risk
Overview

Taxfix is a German fintech/tax prospect in an extended pilot that is struggling. The platform currently covers only 20% of their steering needs. Singular integration is WIP and data discrepancies are a blocker. Snowflake integration was rushed specifically for this pilot. The CMO wants an honest Clarisights vs Looker vs GA4 comparison with "no Sales BS" (described as "Dutch directness"). Philip identified Taxfix's AI ask (budget pacing alerts) as having "the most merit" among customer AI requests.

Timeline
  • Pilot extended — only covering 20% of steering needs
  • Singular integration WIP — data discrepancy blocker
  • Snowflake integration rushed specifically for Taxfix
  • CMO wants honest Clarisights vs Looker vs GA4 comparison — "no Sales BS"
  • Philip: Taxfix's budget pacing alerts AI ask has "the most merit"
  • AI demand: Taxfix wants AI alerts for budget pacing
Key Quotes
  • Sylvia: "He is Dutch and would appreciate no 'Sales BS'." (about Taxfix CMO)
  • Philip: "Taxfix idea has the most merit." (on budget pacing alerts as AI feature)
Product Issues
  • Snowflake integration: Rushed for Taxfix. New capability that drove momentum but needs polish
  • Data trust: Data discrepancies are a blocker. Only covering 20% of steering needs
  • AI/budget pacing: Taxfix wants AI-driven budget pacing alerts — Philip says this has the most merit among AI asks

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · prospect · none risk
Overview

Seven.One (ProSieben) is a German media company in discovery stage. The entry angles are creative analytics and Adjust alternative (Adjust raised prices, pushing Seven.One to evaluate alternatives via AppsFlyer). Clemens is working the prospect. Parship is being used as reference. Part of the creative analytics pipeline.

Timeline
  • Discovery stage
  • Creative analytics + Adjust alternative angles
  • Adjust price increase driving evaluation of alternatives
  • Clemens prospecting
  • Parship used as reference account
Key Quotes

None directly attributed.

Product Issues
  • Creative analytics: Product falls short — Philip's warning about overselling applies

LOST. 4 months invested. Segwise fulfilled P0 criteria without contract. Product gaps: creative-analytics. Competitors: segwise.
DealAmountPipelineStageOwnerCreatedLast Note#NotesLost Reason
Lion Studios - NB 11 2024 $79K New Business Closed Lost Jan Rixgens 2024-11-20 2025-05-08 58 Waiting until one supplier has all features

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · lost · none risk
Overview

Lion Studios is a LOST gaming deal where Clarisights invested 4 months. Segwise, a competitor, fulfilled all P0 criteria without requiring a contract commitment — moving faster on niche features. Arun's reaction: "would not lose a lot of sleep." The loss highlights the competitive risk of slower product delivery in the gaming vertical, specifically around creative analytics.

Timeline
  • 4 months invested in the deal
  • Segwise competitor fulfilled P0 criteria without requiring contract
  • Deal lost to Segwise
  • Arun: "would not lose a lot of sleep" — low strategic value
  • Monodeep and Ritvik were supporting the account
Key Quotes
  • Arun: "would not lose a lot of sleep" (on losing Lion Studios)
Product Issues
  • Creative analytics: Product fell short on critical workflows for gaming use case

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · prospect · none risk
Overview

Fressnapf is a German pet retail company that previously evaluated both Clarisights and Adverity but bought neither solution. Re-connected at the Exactag Dusseldorf event with Thea (Head of Performance Marketing). The previous evaluation means they're familiar with both products and had objections to both — understanding those objections is critical for re-engagement.

Timeline
  • Previously evaluated Clarisights vs Adverity — bought neither
  • Re-connected at Exactag Dusseldorf event with Thea (Head of Perf Marketing)
Key Quotes

None.

Product Issues

No specific issues flagged — need to understand why they rejected both solutions previously.

Open in HubSpot →
Strategic long game. Marketing Services unit (40K+ people, $100M+ DACH P&L). Potential reseller partnership.
DealAmountPipelineStageOwnerCreatedLast Note#NotesLost Reason
Mastercard $500K New Business Closed Lost Arun / Avi 2023-02-26 2023-03-01 8 Were implementing Adverity

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · prospect · none risk
Overview

Mastercard is a strategic long-game partnership opportunity, not a typical customer deal. The Mastercard Marketing Services unit has 40,000+ people worldwide with $100M+ DACH P&L. They have a product called "Mixed Media Optimizer" (MMM model) that Arun and Jan got a demo of. The opportunity is to become a reseller of Mastercard services and get introduced to their finance/fintech network. Mastercard is building a data partnership platform where Clarisights could be a reseller. This could be a category-changing partnership if MMM integration works, opening the entire finance/fintech vertical.

Timeline
  • Arun + Jan got demo of Mastercard's "Mixed Media Optimizer" (MMM model)
  • Contacts: Bojana (Partnerships), Thomas (Sales) — both Frankfurt-based
  • Meeting Oct 21 with Sylvia, Benno, Arun
  • Obi = potential joint upsell case
  • Mastercard building data partnership platform — Clarisights could be reseller
Key Quotes

None directly attributed.

Product Issues

No product issues — this is a partnership play, not a product sale.

Wolt 2022 $400K New Business Closed Lost Arun / Avi 2022-11-21 2023-02-15 5

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · nurturing · medium risk
Overview

Wolt is a European delivery company in nurturing stage. The champion (Jay) has left, creating a stall in the relationship. Their pain points are well-documented: channel silos, cross-channel visibility, and ad-hoc data requests. Currently uses Funnel + Looker + AppsFlyer stack. Was invited to AppsFlyer joint dinner in Berlin (AF side). Represents a strong fit for Clarisights' core value prop but needs re-engagement after champion departure.

Timeline
  • Nurturing stage — champion (Jay) left
  • Pain documented: channel silos, cross-channel visibility, ad-hoc data requests
  • Current stack: Funnel + Looker + AppsFlyer
  • Invited to AppsFlyer joint dinner in Berlin (AF side)
Key Quotes

None directly attributed.

Product Issues

No specific product issues flagged — pain is in their current stack, not Clarisights product.

DealAmountPipelineStageOwnerCreatedLast Note#NotesLost Reason
Lufthansa DE 2021 $20K New Business Closed Lost Owner 40361673 2019-12-10 2020-03-13 3

Narrative (from customer file)

Tier 3 · prospect · none risk
Overview

Publicis (agency) and Lufthansa (enterprise brand) are being pursued as a combo deal. Demo has been scheduled. Sylvia is managing the relationship. Lufthansa is interested, and the aviation vertical represents a new industry signal for Clarisights. Agency + enterprise brand deals have different dynamics — agency becomes the operational user while the brand is the budget holder.

Timeline
  • Demo scheduled — Lufthansa interested
  • Sylvia managing the relationship
  • Agency + enterprise brand combo deal structure
  • Aviation = new vertical signal for Clarisights
Key Quotes

None directly attributed.

Product Issues

No product issues flagged (pre-demo).
